Would you go here?

transcription.text: Looking for a charming beach town to visit? Look no further than Venice, Florida. Venice is located on the southwest coast of Florida about an hour south of Tampa. This area I'm circling in red is called the Island of Venice by the locals and it's my favorite spot. It's where you'll find the historic downtown area with lots of great restaurants, cafes, bars, and cute shops. This is also where you'll find some of Venice's best beaches such as Caspersen Beach, great for snorkeling and finding shark's teeth, and Venice Beach with miles of sandy beach and it's less than a half a mile from downtown. There's also a dog beach here called Brohard Paw Park and a beach the locals call Sharky's Beach which has two beachfront restaurants and the Venice fishing pier. Another attraction to the area is the banyan tree lined Venice Avenue which runs from downtown to Venice Beach. This picturesque street is dotted with historic homes and a beautiful walking path, great for a relaxing stroll from downtown to the beach just in time for sunset. Save this post and send it to a friend you would want to go here with and follow along for more great Florida content from a local.
